rules.description: Modules must not form circular dependencies.
rules.what: Detects import cycles across the module dependency graph.
rules.why: Cycles couple modules, block refactoring, and hide design flaws.

rules.description: Every module should be reachable from the graph.
rules.what: Flags modules that nothing imports (excluding entrypoints).
rules.why: Orphan modules are usually dead code or a missing wiring.

rules.description: Higher layers may import lower layers, never the reverse.
rules.what: Enforces a declared layer order over path globs.
rules.why: Layering keeps dependencies flowing one direction so the core stays clean.

rules.description: Declared module pairs must never depend on each other.
rules.what: Flags any import that matches a forbidden from/to path pair.
rules.why: Explicit forbidden edges protect boundaries from accidental coupling.

rules.description: Declared entrypoints must exist on disk.
rules.what: Verifies each declared entrypoint path is present.
rules.why: A missing entrypoint means the declared architecture is already wrong.
